2604.20229.title: Enhancing Speaker Verification with Whispered Speech via Post-Processing
2604.20229.paper_summary: Speaker verification is a task of confirming an individual's identity through the analysis of their voice. Whispered speech differs from phonated speech in acoustic characteristics, which degrades the performance of speaker verification systems in real-life scenarios, including avoiding fully phonated speech to protect privacy, disrupt others, or when the lack of full vocalization is dictated by a disease. In this paper we propose a model with a training recipe to obtain more robust representations against whispered speech hindrances. The proposed system employs an encoder--decoder structure built atop a fine-tuned speaker verification backbone, optimized jointly using cosine similarity--based classification and triplet loss. We gain relative improvement of 22.26\% compared to the baseline (baseline 6.77\% vs ours 5.27\%) in normal vs whispered speech trials, achieving AUC of 98.16\%. In tests comparing whispered to whispered, our model attains an EER of 1.88\% with AUC equal to 99.73\%, which represents a 15\% relative enhancement over the prior leading ReDimNet-B2. We also offer a summary of the most popular and state-of-the-art speaker verification models in terms of their performance with whispered speech. Additionally, we evaluate how these models perform under noisy audios, obtaining that generally the same relative level of noise degrades the performance of speaker verification more significantly on whispered speech than on normal speech.
2604.20229.paper_summary_zh: 說話者驗證是一項通過分析個體的聲音來確認其身份的任務。<lb/>低聲說話在聲學特徵上與發聲說話有所不同，這在現實場景中降低了說話者驗證系統的性能，包括為了保護隱私、打擾他人或因疾病導致缺乏完全發聲而避免完全發聲的情況。<lb/>在本文中，我們提出了一個模型及其訓練配方，以獲得對低聲說話干擾的更穩健的表示。<lb/>所提出的系統採用了基於精細調整的說話者驗證骨幹的編碼器-解碼器結構，並使用基於餘弦相似度的分類和三元組損失進行聯合優化。<lb/>在正常與低聲說話的試驗中，我們相對於基線（基線 6.77\% vs 我們 5.27\%）獲得了 22.26\% 的相對提升，達到 98.16\% 的 AUC。<lb/>在比較低聲對低聲的測試中，我們的模型達到了 1.88\% 的 EER，AUC 為 99.73\%，這比之前領先的 ReDimNet-B2 提升了 15\%。<lb/>我們還提供了在低聲說話性能方面最受歡迎和最先進的說話者驗證模型的摘要。<lb/>此外，我們評估了這些模型在噪音音頻下的表現，發現一般相同的噪音相對水平在低聲說話上的性能下降比在正常說話上更為顯著。
2604.20229.author: Magdalena Gołębiowska et.al.
